channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: The Growth of US Military Influence in Ghana and Its Implications
item.description: In 2008, US President George W. Bush dismissed concerns about American military bases in Africa as 'baloney.' Yet, by 2018, Ghana had ratified a Defence Cooperation Agreement with the US, granting uni...

item.title: Exploring Motherhood as a Philosophical Subject
item.description: This essay examines the historical neglect of motherhood as a philosophical topic, highlighting how key female thinkers have navigated social and intellectual barriers to contribute insights on matern...

item.title: Royal children help name baby kangaroo as part of conservation effort at Australia Zoo
item.description: The Prince and Princess of Wales, along with their children Prince George, Princess Charlotte and Prince Louis, have taken part in a conservation-themed initiative by naming a baby eastern grey kangar...

item.title: International Efforts Underway to Contain Hantavirus Outbreak Linked to Cruise Ship
item.description: Health authorities around the world are urgently tracking a hantavirus outbreak linked to the cruise ship MV Hondius. The World Health Organization (WHO) confirmed five infections among those connecte...

item.title: One year after Operation Sindoor: how India’s precision strikes hit terror hubs deep inside Pakistan
item.description: One year after Operation Sindoor, a detailed target‑by‑target walkthrough by former Indian Air Force Vice Chief Air Marshal Narmdeshwar Tiwari explains how India responded to the Pahalgam terror attac...

item.title: Authorities seize over 8 kilos of meth from two women at NAIA Terminal 1
item.description: Authorities at Ninoy Aquino International Airport (NAIA) successfully intercepted over eight kilograms of methamphetamine hydrochloride, locally known as shabu, from two women arriving from Phnom Penh...

item.title: Quezon City court denies request to block House subpoena for Mans Carpio's income tax returns
item.description: The Quezon City Regional Trial Court recently rejected the petition filed by Mans Carpio, husband of Vice President Sara Duterte, seeking to prevent the House of Representatives panel from issuing a s...

item.title: Davido inducted into Black Music & Entertainment Walk of Fame 2026 alongside global icons
item.description: Nigerian Afrobeats superstar Davido don achieve another big milestone as dem name am among the 2026 inductees for the Black Music and Entertainment Walk of Fame for Atlanta. Dis recognition put am sid...

item.title: Escalation in Strait of Hormuz as US and Iranian Forces Exchange Strikes Amid Diplomatic Efforts
item.description: Tensions between the United States and Iran have escalated sharply in the Strait of Hormuz, with both nations reporting military strikes against each other. Iran claims that the US violated a ceasefir...

item.title: Police Enter SDP Secretariat During Presidential Candidate Screening, Raising Concerns About Political Pressure
item.description: Today, tension rise for SDP national secretariat for Abuja as security men waka enter di place shortly after former SDP presidential candidate, Prince Adewole Adebayo, finish him screening. People wey...

item.title: When to use at-home flu tests and when to see a GP
item.description: If you start feeling unwell with symptoms like a sore throat, sniffles, or body aches, using a rapid antigen test (RAT) at home can be a quick and convenient way to check for flu or other respiratory ...

item.title: Shakira and Burna Boy Announce Official FIFA World Cup 2026 Song 'Dai Dai'
item.description: Colombian superstar Shakira has revealed a teaser for her new song 'Dai Dai,' the official anthem for the FIFA World Cup 2026, which she will perform alongside Nigerian Afrobeats sensation Burna Boy. ...

item.title: Quezon City Court Dismisses Petition to Stop Impeachment Proceedings Against VP Sara Duterte
item.description: The Quezon City Regional Trial Court (RTC) has dismissed the petition filed by lawyer Manases Carpio, husband of Vice President Sara Duterte, seeking to stop the ongoing impeachment proceedings agains...

item.title: Three Women Charged in Australia Following Return from Syrian Refugee Camp
item.description: Three women returning from a refugee camp in Syria were arrested in Australia yesterday evening and face a range of charges, including involvement in slavery. Authorities confirmed the arrests upon th...

item.title: Three Canadians Isolating at Home After Hantavirus Outbreak on Cruise Ship
item.description: Three Canadians are currently in isolation in Ontario and Quebec following a hantavirus outbreak on the Dutch cruise ship MV Hondius. The cruise, which included 88 passengers and 59 crew members from ...

item.title: Lesotho Nationals Advised to Carry IDs as Protests Against Foreigners Continue in SA
item.description: The High Commission of Lesotho has issued an alert urging its nationals in South Africa to exercise caution amidst ongoing protests targeting undocumented foreigners. Lesotho citizens are advised to a...

item.title: Cyberattack compromises Australian school student data
item.description: A significant cybersecurity breach has occurred, with a criminal group claiming responsibility for accessing the personal data of Australian school students through a hack on Instructure, an online le...

item.title: NITI Aayog Vice Chairman’s Phone Hacked, Fake Money Requests Circulate on WhatsApp
item.description: On Thursday evening, Ashok Kumar Lahiri, the Vice Chairman of NITI Aayog, reported that his personal phone number was hacked. According to a Facebook post by Lahiri, the hacker used his number to send...

item.title: Sukanya’s Journey Back to Her Ancestral Home in Meerut
item.description: The excerpt from Parul Sharma’s novel 'The Missing Piece' narrates the story of Shiladitya, a man living in London, and the arrival of his daughter, Sukanya, on a freezing winter night. Shiladitya fon...

item.title: Subway Service Restored on Part of Line 2 Following Temporary Closure
item.description: Toronto's subway Line 2 resumed regular service after experiencing a three-hour disruption earlier today. The temporary closure was prompted by an incident involving a motorcyclist who was seriously i...
